mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals durable consumption momentum that lifts expected corporate revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 368233 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the estimated coefficient on 3-month RSAFS growth is nonpositive, or if triggered positive signals produce below-baseline 20-day returns in more than 55% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0% raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, reducing valuation multiples and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.25 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of cases or if the FEDFUNDS signal has a nonnegative return coefficient after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals a housing-led demand slowdown that weakens construction, durable goods, and credit-sensitive earnings, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1795 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day returns following housing-start breakdowns are not lower than unconditional 60-day returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 50% over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate after a low-unemployment expansion signals labor-market deterioration and falling cyclical earnings expectations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases paired with weak PAYEMS growth do not precede lower 60-day returns, or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI with an already high federal funds rate indicates persistent inflation pressure that keeps policy restrictive and compresses equity multiples, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 203.7 as of t-1mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.25 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL acceleration and high-FEDFUNDS interaction fails to predict lower 20-day returns, or if its walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three straight years.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals stronger real output and improving operating leverage for cyclical firms, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 113.7063 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by nonpositive average 5-day returns or if the signal coefficient is negative in most rolling 2-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with slowing CPI identifies a disinflationary expansion in which real activity improves while discount-rate pressure eases, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month growth decelerates
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-positive and CPI-decelerating state does not deliver higher 60-day returns than either condition alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth with unemployment below 5% and FEDFUNDS above 5% signals late-cycle wage pressure that prolongs restrictive policy, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive, UNRATE is below 5.0%, and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 135500 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if this three-series late-cycle state produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences or if removing FEDFUNDS does not reduce predictive performance.
